threadTitle: Things that instantly reveal you just moved here
body: You still call it 'the Tube' instead of 'the Underground.' Everyone here knows it's just the Overground after a while.
Caught a glimpse of two mates sharing a burrito and laughing so hard on the 133, nearly missed their stop at Coldharbour Lane. Classic Brixton night out.

threadTitle: When you knew you were actually living here
body: Last night, I heard a group of kids turn a bus stop into a stage, dancing to their own beats while waiting for the delayed 104. Only in Neukölln.

threadTitle: Things Berlin does that nowhere else does
body: Only in Kreuzberg do you find a Kotti kebab stand where live music blares and locals debate the best falafel toppings at 3 AM. It's a vibe.

threadTitle: Real signs someone is from here vs just working here
body: You know a local when they know to stock up on snacks before a BART closure; it’s always the same story on Fridays.

threadTitle: Muni moments that define this city
body: Yesterday, a guy with a pet parrot in a basket boarded the 38. The parrot squawked louder than the engine. Only in the Tenderloin, right?

threadTitle: Things tourists get wrong about living here
body: Tourists don’t realize the locals actually avoid Park Güell. Too many people, overpriced tapas, and it’s not even the best view of the city.

threadTitle: How to spot a recent arrival
body: They always hold their map upside down, looking bewildered at the closest metro entrance, while locals breeze by like they’re in a race.

threadTitle: Things that instantly reveal you just moved here
body: When you still ask for a latte at the coffee shop instead of knowing it’s a flat white you’re after. Total giveaway.
Last night on the 133, a guy offered his takeaway to a jammed full bus. Turns out it was from the best Caribbean spot in Brixton. Talk about a journey.

threadTitle: When you knew you were actually living here
body: The moment I spotted a guy painting a mural on a crumbling wall while two kids played footy on the street – that’s Neukölln for you.

threadTitle: Things Berlin does that nowhere else does
body: Only in Kreuzberg can you grab a currywurst at 3 AM after an underground rave and then chill by the canal with a random group of artists.

threadTitle: Real signs someone is from here vs just working here
body: You know someone’s local when they grimace at BART delays but still know exactly which taco truck to hit for a quick bite.

threadTitle: Muni moments that define this city
body: Just saw a Muni bus turn the corner, but the driver had to pause for a street performer juggling flaming clubs—only in SF, right?

threadTitle: Things tourists get wrong about living here
body: Tourists think the street performers are just part of the plaza vibe, but locals know they’re often a cover for pickpockets. Keeps us on our toes.

threadTitle: How to spot a recent arrival
body: They’re the ones still taking photos of the Sagrada Família from every angle, oblivious to the fact that the real beauty is found in the narrow streets of Gràcia.
